summary_one_line: Input must parse into a namespaced XML document whose freed doc carries a non-NULL oldNs reconciled-namespace list.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document that parses successfully AND declares/uses namespaces (xmlns / prefixed names like xlink) so that xmlReadMemory builds a document whose cur->oldNs reconciled-namespace list is non-NULL at xmlFreeDoc time.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry a valid XML prolog plus namespace markup (xmlns(, xlink, CDATA) and the winner-side coverage runs the namespace-splitting machinery heavily (xmlSplitQName2 106 vs 0, xmlSplitQName3 33 vs 0, xmlNewNsPropEatName 30 vs 0), so the parsed doc accumulates old namespaces and the true branch is taken (W T=8 F=0 at L1248).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are smaller/garbled (leading binary bytes, mangled http:////// runs, broken tag fragments) that never build a document with a populated oldNs, so the namespace functions get zero invocations on the L side and the branch stays false (L T=0 F=20 at L1248).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads logged CMP operands and substitutes them at matching input offsets, repairing the literal string/structural comparands (xmlns prefixes, attribute keywords, the version/DOCTYPE prolog) that gate a successful namespaced parse; naive lacks I2S and must hit those multi-byte literals by blind havoc, so it almost never assembles a document that populates oldNs.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side at the blocker line is the namespace-list-free guard cur->oldNs != NULL.
The winner takes the true branch in all resolving runs while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1  L1248  T=8 F=0  T=0 F=20  if (cur->oldNs != NULL) xmlFreeNsList(cur->oldNs);

evidence_trail.claim: Namespace-splitting functions run heavily for the winner and not at all for the loser, evidencing namespaced-document construction.
Namespace-property construction (xmlNewNsPropEatName) is exercised only on the winner side.
Winner-resolving seeds contain a valid XML prolog and namespace markup (xmlns / xlink).
Loser-blocking seeds contain garbled non-XML byte runs rather than well-formed namespaced markup.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at input offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, so it must reach literal/structural comparands by blind byte mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6930 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 51fd9b36079604e3`; the ancestor chain shows 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 and depth 4, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that parses a namespace-free but otherwise valid XML document still taking the true branch (oldNs populated) would refute the namespace requirement; equivalently, cmplog failing to out-resolve naive on a target whose only gate is a non-CMP structural namespace property would refute the I2S attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: It is inferred but not directly shown that cur->oldNs is populated specifically by namespace reconciliation rather than by some other code path also exercised only by the winner.
